phd.title_en: Experimental research of non-locality of turbulence in magnetized plasma.
phd.title_jp: 磁化プラズマにおける乱流の大域性の実験研究
phd.abstract_en: Understanding the physical mechanism of turbulent transport in magnetized plasmas and suppressing it is essential for the realization of fusion reactors, which are expected to be the next generation large-scale power plants. In magnetized plasmas, microscopic turbulence coexists and interacts with macroscopic structures and flows, resulting in the development of non-locality. PLATO at the Research Institute of Applied Mechanics, Kyushu University is the first tokamak specific to turbulence measurements and has a lot of measurement ports. In this research, the role of non-locality in turbulent transport will be studied by using tomography measurement of the global structure and a newly developed composite probe array.

phd.title_en: A carbon footprint analysis considering the production activities of the informal sector
phd.title_jp: インフォーマルセクターの生産活動を考慮したカーボンフットプリントの実証分析
phd.abstract_en: The informal sector is a set of activities that are imperfectly regulated by the governments, and it is hardly accounted on official statistics. In emerging and developing countries, such unofficial economic activities generate one-third of the total GDP, hence the informal sector is the vital for supply chains in the regions. On the other hand, many study have demonstrated that the higher informality of economies have negative impacts on environment. This study is the first attempt to construct an extended input-output table that distinguishes the formal sector and the informal sector to explore how the informal sector contributes carbon footprint of the country.

phd.title_en: CO2 mitigation through global supply chain restructuring
phd.title_jp: グローバルサプライチェーンの再構築を通したCO2排出削減
phd.abstract_en: Automotive industries have large CO 2 emissions from their global supply chain. In order to realize a decarbonized society, there is urgent need for automotive industries to build a green supply chain, taking the opportunity of the restructuring of GSC in major countries caused by COVID-19. To build a green supply chain, it is important for an industry to identify the CO 2 emission hotspots in the its GSC and to reduce CO 2 emissions from these hotspots intensively. Therefore, this study develops a new frame work that can estimate the impact of the restructuring of Japan’s automotive industry which eliminates the CO 2 emission hotspots on the global carbon footprint, and proposes appropriate climate policies for the restructuring to reduce comprehensive CO 2 emissions from the newly reformed GSC of Japan’s automotive industry.

phd.title_en: Environmental sustainability assessment using high-resolution spatial information
phd.title_jp: 空間情報を用いた高分解能での環境持続可能性評価
phd.abstract_en: The purpose of this study is to develop a quantitative index that can be used as a material for considering the trade-off between the introduction of renewable energy and the preservation of ecosystems on high (250m x 250m) resolution. Based on spatial information and information on revealed and stated preferences of households and industrial sectors, we will evaluate the determinants of preferences for renewable energy and ecosystems using contingent valuation method, conjoint analysis, and multi-criteria decision analysis. Based on this, we will predict the dynamics of renewable energy and ecosystems, and construct and evaluate an environmental sustainability assessment system.

phd.title_en: Addressing fuel starvation based degradation in polymer electrolyte fuel cells
phd.title_jp: Addressing fuel starvation based degradation in polymer electrolyte fuel cells
phd.abstract_en: I am excited to be part of the movement that is bringing hydrogen based technology to society. It is inevitable that hydrogen based fuel cell vehicles will occupy a strong place in future society. For now, improving fuel cell durability and performance while also driving down costs remains to be the last technological obstacle before widespread adoption. The anode side of the fuel cell is especially prone to degradation due to fuel starvation, which occurs under extreme operating conditions. I am currently working on reducing the anode degradation modes at high humidity operating conditions by developing novel catalyst and gas diffusion layers. Ideally, by using a reversal tolerant, non-platinum based catalyst and a surface modified Titanium mesh based gas diffusion layer, I hope to achieve low cost, high performance and durable materials for automobile fuel cell applications.

phd.title_en: Curved Organic Molecules as Electrode Active Material
phd.title_jp: 湾曲活物質を用いた電池の創成
phd.abstract_en: Na-ion batteries are expected to be a promising candidate for post-Li ion batteries due to the abundance of Na resources. However, since the ionic volume of Na is more than twice that of Li, the same materials for Li-ion batteries cannot be used for Na-ion batteries. Organic electrode materials are suitable for hosting the insertion of bulky Na ions due to their low specific gravity. In this research, I focus on organic π-conjugated materials with curvature. I have already found that sumanenetrione, which has a bowl-shaped structure, can be used as an active material for Na ion batteries. Further investigation on the storage of other metal ions and the use of other curved active materials will be conducted.

phd.title_en: Electrochemical CO 2 Conversion to Value-added Chemicals
phd.title_jp: 電気化学二酸化炭素を付加価値化学物質に変換する
phd.abstract_en: Electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 into C 2+ products is a highly attractive route for CO 2 utilization and decreasing global warming. However, the selectivity and yield of C 2+ products remains low. Here I focus on the catalyst development and setup design to increase selectivity and yield of C 2+ products via electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 .

phd.title_en: The impact of compact cities on carbon emissions
phd.title_jp: The impact of compact cities on carbon emissions
phd.abstract_en: Cities play an important role in greenhouse gas emissions. In recent years, ideas about sustainable urban development have evolved from the point of view of low-carbon cities, in which the concept of a ‘compact city’ has been proposed. After conducting a thorough literature review, I identified that previous studies have mainly concentrated on the definition, formation, characteristics, quantification, and policy evaluation of compact cities. But there is still a gap in the academic knowledge about how compact cities have impact on urban sustainability, especially on carbon emission reduction. The study will attach importance to policy-driven compact cities, combining literature review, case study and empirical research to examine the relationship and to clarify the impact mechanisms between various aspects of compact cities and carbon emissions.

phd.title_en: Breakthrough of 900MPa barrier of fatigue crack growth resistance in BCC steels in high-pressure hydrogen environment
phd.title_jp: Breakthrough of 900MPa barrier of fatigue crack growth resistance in BCC steels in high-pressure hydrogen environment
phd.abstract_en: Hydrogen is promised an important future with a key role for a carbon-free society. However, it is often responsible for the degradation of strength properties of the material storing it, leading to limited storing capacity and safety issues. The goal of the research is to increase the resistance of low-alloy steels to fatigue crack growth rate in hydrogen environment. Doing so will allow the design of safer and higher-capacity hydrogen storage containers. Improving the materials’ strengths will be done through microstructure refinement (ausforming process). This research will be the first to study fatigue crack growth properties in hydrogen gas on ausformed low-alloy steels.

phd.title_en: The research on ventilation control of ship engine room considering ammonia fuel
phd.title_jp: アンモニア燃料を考慮した船舶機関室の換気制御に関する研究
phd.abstract_en: Greenhouse gas emissions from ships, especially international shipping, have been a major factor in global warming and IMO set a goal to reduce GHG emissions from international shipping. Ammonia fuel is one of the next-generation marine fuels that realizes decarbonization. On the other hand, ammonia has strong toxicity. Hence strict measures are required in terms of risk management, and regulations regarding ammonia as a fuel are being developed. In this study, to present an evaluation index for rule development, we analyse the risk of ammonia leakage in the ship engine room and optimize the ventilation control in the case of ammonia leakage by numerical fluid calculation and model experiments.

phd.abstract_en: Overall water splitting using a photocatalyst and solar energy has attracted significant attention as a next-generation energy medium, but the current light-hydrogen conversion efficiency is about 1%. Therefore, I will focus on nanosheet structures which can be improved the surface area, photon absorption efficiency, and dye loading amount. In this research, I will develop an exfoliation reagent that has a dye-sensitizing ability, and a hybrid photocatalyst with a nanosheet type photocatalyst system that can respond to near infrared region.

phd.title_en: Performance study of a passive dehumidification system naturally controls the indoor hygrothermal environment using renewable energy.
phd.title_jp: 再生可能エネルギーを利用して室内温湿度を自然調節するPSEシステムの開発
phd.abstract_en: To improve the energy-saving performance of passive dehumidification and solar collection (PDSC) envelope system, which naturally cools and dehumidifies indoor air in summer and collects solar heat in winter, we develop a novel PSE (PDSC system combined with energy recovery ventilation) to reduce heat loads of air-conditioning system. Based on the results of numerical experiments of PSE conducted so far, we will jointly develop PSE equipment with private companies to commercialize it for general use. In addition, we will conduct outdoor experiments in a demonstration house and numerical simulations to verify efficient operating conditions and methods for PSE throughout the year and design an optimal control system.

phd.title_en: CO 2 Reduction Potential of Expanding Car Sharing Services
phd.title_jp: カーシェアリング普及のCO2排出削減効果
phd.abstract_en: While it is important to change the way we use cars in order to decarbonize, there is little research on the environmental impact of car sharing services, which significantly change the way we use cars. We analyze how CO 2 emissions in Japan will change when car sharing services become widespread, and make recommendations that will lead to global warming mitigation policies. This study applies a stock-flow model of passenger cars to develop a framework that can comprehensively analyze changes in CO 2 emissions when car-sharing services expand, and quantitatively analyze the role of the car-sharing service diffusion in reducing CO 2 emissions.

phd.title_en: Development of Advanced Computer Simulated Person for Indoor Thermal Comfort and Air Quality Assessment
phd.title_jp: Development of Advanced Computer Simulated Person for Indoor Thermal Comfort and Air Quality Assessment
phd.abstract_en: Many comprehensive evaluation methods for IEQ (Indoor Environmental Quality) using numerical analysis such as Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) have been proposed. Also, the reproduction of human in indoor space is regarded as a key point to evaluate IEQ with high accuracy. Against this background, the Computer-Simulated Person (CSP), which can treat specific characteristics of the human body, has been suggested as a “digital twin” for CFD simulation. However, previous research simplified or ignored the clothing on CSP and heat and mass transfer around cloth. So, my research aims to develop a new CSP with clothing and advanced estimation method of IEQ. This research focused on the 3 major tasks: 1) To establish an advanced thermal comfort analysis method with clothing 2) Integration of developed CSP with the human airway model 3) To suggest energy-efficient HVAC control method using the developed CSP.

Hydrogen utilization and CO 2 conversion to functional products are two alternatives to reduce emissions. Both processes can be carried out through photocatalysis which uses light in the presence of a semiconductor to achieve a chemical reaction. Among several materials, high entropy ceramics demonstrated good properties as photocatalysts. Objective: A. Design new high entropy ceramics with outstanding photocatalytic activity. B. phd.title_en: Data-Driven Design of Carbon Materials for Hydrogen Storage
phd.title_jp: Data-Driven Design of Carbon Materials for Hydrogen Storage
phd.abstract_en: In the transport sector, decarbonization is mainly done by converting combustion vehicles to battery electric vehicles. However, the low gravimetric energy capacity and long recharging times of batteries necessitate another solution for heavy duty transport. Hydrogen can fulfill this role due to its high energy density and quick refueling capability. One remaining issue is its storage: hydrogen has a low volumetric density. Thus, it needs to be either compressed to 70 MPa or liquefied to be stored on-board. Unfortunately, these solutions still suffer from low efficiency and high costs. I study an alternative method of hydrogen storage: densifying hydrogen by adsorbing it onto high surface area carbons. This method is cheaper than 70 MPa compression, and it loses less energy than liquefaction. To further improve the materials used, I use data science techniques to clearly quantify the processing-structure-property relationships affecting the hydrogen uptake performance of high surface area carbon materials.

phd.abstract_en: 本研究の目的は、将来の建物のエネルギーシミュレーションにおいて、人間の意識や行動の変化が及ぼす影響をシミュレートするためのツールを開発することである。本研究ではまず、アンケートと検証実験により、人間の環境心理と行動の関係の数理モデルを作る。そして、異なる人々に、環境意識を高めて省エネ行動を誘発するための異なる手段を設定する。これをもとに人間の行動をモデル化し、エネルギー消費のシミュレーションと組み合わせる。最後に、人間の意識や行動がエネルギー消費に与える影響を分析するシミュレーションツールを開発する。

phd.title_jp: Realization of decarbonization coexisting with food production: Creation of environmentally resilient biomass energy resource crops through photosynthetic modification based on functional genomics.
phd.abstract_en: This study aims to utilize stress-tolerant plants as alternative biofuel crops grown in non-crop areas to secure stable biomass energy resource crops and create new resource crops resilient to environmental stress. We will use bioinformatics to comprehensively analyze the structure and function of genes and non-genetic regions related to high-stress tolerance and photosynthetic conversion mechanisms and to modify the genes and non-translational regions in the target plants. In addition, we will establish a cultivation method to enhance the components of biofuels and other raw materials and establish a production method for biofuel crops that does not compete with food crops.

phd.abstract_en: To achieve a carbon-free society, the development of excellent secondary batteries is one of the priority issues. Li-ion batteries and Li-Air batteries are attracting attention due to their high energy densities. Moreover, an all-solid-state battery based on Li conductive oxide is expected as the next-generation battery with high energy density and high safety. Here, in this study, I aim to develop new materials that show high electronic-Li-ionic conductivity. I am trying to establish the material design for excellent mixed conductors by a design of experiments and apply those materials for battery applications.

phd.title_jp: 住宅ストックの動態を考慮した包括的LCA評価フレームワークの開発と実証分析
phd.abstract_en: CO 2 emissions from the housing and building sectors in Japan are said to account for about 40% of the total. While various measures have been taken to reduce CO 2 emissions, a detailed analysis of the dichotomous relationship between the reduction of CO 2 emissions during construction and the increase during use by extending the service life of houses, and the increase of CO 2 emissions during construction and the reduction during use by the spread of energy-saving houses has not yet been conducted. This study estimates the life-cycle CO 2 emissions of Japanese housing as a whole, taking into account the life span of the building as a whole and the composition of the stock by age, and investigates the trade-off relationship that occurs with the proliferation of longer-lived and energy-efficient housing through scenario analysis.

phd.abstract_en: So far, it has been clarified that when we use the high-capacity negative electrode materials such as Mg(BH 4 ) 2 , Ca(BH 4 ) 2 , and MgH 2 , solid electrolytes can be generated by electrode reaction. As the electrode reaction products of MgH 2 , LiH and Mg will form by Li insertion, and despite the low Li ion conductivity of LiH, it can be used as an excellent negative electrode of all-solid-state battery due to the self-generation of solid electrolyte. In this study, it is necessary to clarify the ionic conductivity of the formed Li salt and the three-dimensional distribution of reactants, products, and carbon in the electrode mixture during charging and discharging for the further understanding of the factor than affect the battery characteristic of the negative electrode that using the self-generated solid electrolyte.

phd.title_en: Toward Safer, Smarter, and Decarbonized Mobility: Personalized Energy-efficient Driving for Intelligent Vehicles
phd.title_jp: Toward Safer, Smarter, and Decarbonized Mobility: Personalized Energy-efficient Driving for Intelligent Vehicles
phd.abstract_en: Analyzing the energy consumption for road vehicles and the corresponding driving behaviors are critical tasks for the realization of public traffic with a low energy cost and high efficiency. Especially, intelligent and connected vehicular technologies potentiate the energy-saving driving with ubiquitous and sensible traffic information. In this research, we try to utilize the advanced optimal control algorithm to optimize the driving decision and control output from vehicle powertrain so that the relationship between vehicle driving, road condition, traffic situation and powertrain efficiency can be fully matched to realize the energy-efficient driving. Meanwhile, the personalized driving styles are expected to be incorporated into the automated driving control system to quantitatively evaluate the impact from driving style on energy consumption. Ultimately, a human-oriented, safer, smarter and decarbonized energy-efficient autonomous driving system is expected to be developed for the next-generation mobility.

phd.title_en: Exploration of rate-limiting factors and development of design guidelines for MH alloy systems
phd.title_jp: MH合金システムの速度律速因子の探求と設計指針の構築
phd.abstract_en: To achieve the goal of a hydrogen society, a safer, more mass- and volume-efficient, and less expensive method of hydrogen storage is essential. The metal hydride alloys (MH alloys) are a safer alternative to high-pressure tanks, and the first step in using MH alloy systems is to increase the filling and releasing rates from MH alloy tanks. To this end, we will comprehensively examine the processes from the surface of MH alloy particles to the internal diffusion and phase change processes and clarify the rate-limiting factors of the MH alloy system. Then, we optimize the filling and releasing time to minimize the representative times for solid-phase diffusion, gas-phase diffusion, and heat transfer.

phd.title_en: Analysis of Tritium permeation behavior of Li rod simulant for development of Tritium confinement technology
phd.title_jp: トリチウム閉じ込め技術開発に向けた、Liロッド模擬試験体のトリチウム透過挙動解析
phd.abstract_en: Tritium is used as a fuel in fusion reactors, which are expected to be a next-generation energy source. Since the amount of lithium is extremely small when naturally occurring, tritium production using high-temperature gas reactors is being considered. However, tritium retention behavior in materials in high-temperature environments is still unknown, and clarification of these issues is essential from the viewpoint of safety. In this study, tritium permeation experiments will be conducted using a test specimen that simulates the core of a high-temperature gas-cooled reactor. The experimental results will be analyzed from a chemical engineering viewpoint to model the tritium permeation behavior and to elucidate the basic tritium migration phenomena.

phd.title_en: Elucidating the mechanisms of reduce creep life in hydrogen to ensure the safety of advanced high-temperature hydrogen technologies
phd.title_jp: 高温水素利用機器の安全性を確実にするための水素中クリープ寿命低下の機構解明に関する研究
phd.abstract_en: Advanced high-temperature hydrogen technologies, such as SOEC for hydrogen generation and SOFC for hydrogen power generation, are expected to play a significant role in achieving carbon neutrality by 2050. The structural components of these devices are subjected to a very aggressive environment, which is high-temperature and hydrogen. Furthermore, considering creep deformation is essential in designing mechanical components that operate at high temperature. However, the understanding of degradation of structural materials in high-temperature hydrogen remains insufficient. The objective of this study is to elucidate the mechanisms by which hydrogen affects creep deformation. To achieve this, I will collaborate internationally and interdisciplinarily with Professor Kirchheim at the University of Gottingen, who has developed the defactant theory based on thermodynamics for hydrogen embrittlement at room temperature, along with his colleagues.

phd.title_en: An Analysis and System Proposal Regarding Social Acceptance of Geothermal Development with Mathematical-Sociology Approach
phd.title_jp: 地熱発電の社会受容性に関する数理社会学的分析と制度提案
phd.abstract_en: Although Japan has the third-largest geothermal potential in the world, its installed capacity of geothermal power plants ranks in 10th globally. One of the reasons why the geothermal development has been stagnant in Japan is the oppositions from local hot spa owners. This problem is academically classified as social acceptance of geothermal development, and previous studies conducted detailed case studies to find out how to improve the social acceptance and to build a successful consensus among stakeholders. This study aims to suggest effective knowledge and systems contributing to the improvement of social acceptance of geothermal development by applying mathematical-sociology methods such as game theory and agent-based simulation to the analysis of the consensus-building situations.

phd.title_en: Nanoparticle modified oxide as model air electrode for oxygen reduction site
phd.title_jp: Nanoparticle modified oxide as model air electrode for oxygen reduction site
phd.abstract_en: Global warming and climate change induce serious problems in the earth’s life due to the usage of fossil fuels. These life-threatening problems must be solved by replacing fossil fuels to hydrogen, which has an important role to develop a clean, efficient, and environmental-friendly energy society. For green hydrogen production, high-temperature steam electrolysis (HTSE) is expecting to be used through Solid Oxide Electrolysis Cells (SOECs), which is reverse reaction of Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFCs), electrochemical energy-conversion devices that convert chemical energy directly to electrical energy with high efficiency and low pollution. This hybrid energy system requires highly active electrode for ORR/OER to produce hydrogen and electricity efficiently. Therefore, I study to understand active sites for ORR and OER design oxygen dissociation and association reaction. By doing so, it can decrease IR loss and overpotential to boost cell performance as well as improve the reversibility and durability of the single cell.

phd.title_en: Carbon Footprint Analysis of Japanese Wooden Houses
phd.title_jp: 日本の木造住宅のカーボンフットプリント分析
phd.abstract_en: Global construction activities and associated electricity consumption account for 19% of total CO 2 emissions. Most CO 2 emission reduction measures in the Japanese residential sector are not focused on reducing CO 2 emissions from the supply chain. To reduce CO 2 emissions through the supply chain, it is necessary to identify CO 2 emission hotspots in the supply chain structure. Therefore, this study estimates the supply chain structure of wooden houses, which account for 90% of detached houses in Japan, and identifies CO 2 emission hotspots in the supply chain structure.

phd.title_en: Research on the production of high-performance carbon fiber using stranded resources
phd.title_jp: 座礁資源を利用した高機能炭素繊維の製造に関する研究
phd.abstract_en: “Stranded resources” refer to resources that have lost value due to environmental and social changes. An example is ethylene bottom oil (EO), a heavy residue oil discharged during ethylene production. This is currently used as boiler fuel and is responsible for a large amount of CO 2 emissions. Therefore, this study focuses on EO as one of the stranded resources and attempts to make effective use of EO. The production of EO-derived high-performance carbon fiber will contribute to the removal of potential CO 2 emission sources and the construction of an innovative decarbonized society. We will propose a new and unprecedented production method to manufacture high-performance carbon fibers, aiming for low-cost and high-yield production.

phd.title_en: Dramatic improvement of critical current density based on the analogy of boiling and water electrolysis
phd.title_jp: 沸騰と水電解のアナロジーに基づく水電解性能の飛躍的な向上
phd.abstract_en: Renewable energy power generation has received a great deal of attention in recent years. For the demand of using those excess electricity power generated by renewable energy more efficient without wasting, there is a need to develop the alkaline water electrolysis which can transform excess generated electricity into hydrogen, as a large-scale and low-cost electricity storage technology. In the previous research, based on the analogy between boiling and water electrolysis, we applied the cooling method by using honeycomb porous plate, which had succeeded in significantly improving boiling Critical Heat Flux (CHF) to alkaline water electrolysis, and succeeded in improving the Critical Current Density (CCD) of water electrolysis. In order to clarify the mechanism of water electrolysis improvement from microscale, we developed a honeycomb porous electrode creation technology with which honeycomb porous structure can be formed by self-organization process, then plan to build a theoretical model based on the experimental results.

phd.title_en: Carbon footprint analysis for tourism in Japan using environmentally extended input-output approach.
phd.title_jp: 環境・観光分析用産業連関分析を用いた旅行者のカーボンフットプリント分析
phd.abstract_en: Tourism demand in Japan is expected to increase over the long term, and CO 2 emissions from tourism are also expected to increase. This study aims to develop a new input-output framework which can estimate the carbon footprint per inbound traveler by nationality and per domestic traveler by destination. The results can be used to identify travelers and industrial sectors with large CO 2 burdens, and to make demand-side policy recommendations (e.g., a carbon tax on entry into Japan and a carbon labeling system) and supply-side policy recommendations (e.g., carbon subsidies for industries with large CO 2 burdens).

phd.title_en: Electrochemical CO 2 Reduction Catalyst for Multi-carbon Products
phd.title_jp: マルチカーボン生成を目指した電気化学CO₂還元触媒の検討
phd.abstract_en: The large amount of CO 2 emission should be decreased to realize carbon neutral society. One of the technologies to reduce CO 2 emission is CO 2 reduction reaction (CO 2 RR). CO 2 RR is highly attracted because the reaction occurred under mild conditions such as room temperature and atmospheric pressure. However, CO 2 RR needs an efficient electrocatalyst to increase product selectivity and decrease overpotential. In this research, we develop the electrocatalyst by rationally designing a structure and compositing various elements to increase the catalytic activity for CO 2 RR.

phd.title_jp: 再生可能エネルギーを利用して室内温湿度を自然調節するPSEシステムの開発
phd.abstract_en: Japanese government declared goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2050, to achieve this target the Ministry of Energy has proposed the BI-Tech project, which aims to help raise awareness of energy conservation among building occupants through AI and IoT technologies. k2spring.title_en: The Changing Landscape of EV Business Ecosystem: A Downstream Value Chain Analysis
k2spring.title_jp: The Changing Landscape of EV Business Ecosystem: A Downstream Value Chain Analysis
k2spring.abstract_en: This research explores the evolving electric vehicle (EV) business ecosystem, with a focus on downstream value chain activities such as charging infrastructure, after-sales services, and mobility solutions. Unlike traditional vehicles, EVs require new digital and service-oriented capabilities that enable continuous value creation beyond the point of sale. Through a multi-case study approach, the study examines how legacy automakers, start-ups, and cross-industry entrants are reconfiguring strategies to meet changing consumer needs. The research analyses how these actors build ecosystem partnerships, manage customer engagement, and innovate in areas like battery services and platform-based mobility — providing both theoretical insights into capability transformation under technological disruption and practical guidance for building competitive, sustainable business models in the EV era.

phd.title_en: The Changing Landscape of EV Business Ecosystem: A Downstream Value Chain Analysis
phd.title_jp: The Changing Landscape of EV Business Ecosystem: A Downstream Value Chain Analysis
phd.abstract_en: This research explores the evolving EV business ecosystem, with a focus on downstream value chain activities such as charging infrastructure, after-sales services, and mobility solutions. Unlike traditional vehicles, EVs require new digital and service-oriented capabilities that enable continuous value creation beyond the point of sale. Through a multi-case study approach, the study examines how legacy automakers, start-ups, and cross-industry entrants are reconfiguring their resources and strategies to meet changing consumer needs. It analyzes how these actors build ecosystem partnerships, manage customer engagement, and innovate in areas like battery services and platform-based mobility. By investigating these dynamics, the research provides both theoretical insights into capability transformation under technological disruption and practical guidance for building more competitive, sustainable business models in the EV era. Ultimately, it aims to illuminate how digital and service innovations are reshaping the final stage of automotive value delivery to support a user-centric and low-carbon mobility future.

k2spring.title_en: Optimizing heat sink design in nuclear fusion reactors using topology optimisation
k2spring.title_jp: Optimizing heat sink design in nuclear fusion reactors using topology optimisation
k2spring.abstract_en: Plasma-facing components (PFCs) are exposed to extreme heat loads inside a fusion reactor. In a fast-breeder reactor, reduced-activation ferritic steel (RAFS) is used around cooling systems inside PFCs, making heat sink design with consideration of RAFS thermal conductivity particularly important. This study applies topology optimisation to design an effective heat sink geometry inside the reactor, with the goal of maximising heat removal performance under fusion-relevant operating conditions.

This study applies topology optimisation to design an effective heat sink geometry inside the reactor, with the goal of maximising heat removal performance under fusion-relevant operating conditions.

phd.title_en: Optimizing heat sink in nuclear fusion reactor using Topology optimization
phd.title_jp: Optimizing heat sink in nuclear fusion reactor using Topology optimization
phd.abstract_en: Plasma facing components (PFCs) are faced with extreme heat load inside a fusion reactor. Some useful concepts for optimizing heat deposition of PFCs are necessary. In a fast-breeder reactor, reduced-activation ferritic steel (RAFS) will be used around cooling systems inside PFCs. Then it is effective to design a heat sink with consideration about the heat conductivity of RAFS. Topology optimization can play a role to design its heat sink. The goal of this study is to propose a design for an effective heat sink inside reactor using this technique.

k2spring.title_en: Development of Three-Dimensional Chiral Plasmonic Meta-clusters with Circular Polarization Characteristics for Effective Utilization of Sunlight
k2spring.title_jp: Development of Three-Dimensional Chiral Plasmonic Meta-clusters with Circular Polarization Characteristics for Effective Utilization of Sunlight
k2spring.abstract_en: Circular polarisation has potential applications in improving the efficiency of solar cells and promoting plant growth. This research develops high-performance dichroic circular polarisers (DCP) and meta-surfaces to enhance circular polarised photoluminescence (CPL) of chiral chromophores. Three-dimensional helical chiral plasmonic meta-clusters are fabricated by rolling up 2D plasmonic arrays in an origami-like process, demonstrating DCP behaviour and CPL enhancement through unique electromagnetic properties of asymmetric plasmonic nanostructures.

phd.title_en: Development of Three-Dimensional Chiral Plasmonic Meta-clusters with Circular Polarization Characteristics for Effective Utilization of Sunlight
phd.title_jp: Development of Three-Dimensional Chiral Plasmonic Meta-clusters with Circular Polarization Characteristics for Effective Utilization of Sunlight
phd.abstract_en: Circular polarization has potential applications in improving the efficiency of solar cells and promoting plant growth, and the development of high-performance dichroic circular polarizers (DCP) and meta-surfaces to enhance circular polarized photoluminescence (CPL) of chiral chromophores is expected. Asymmetric plasmonic nanostructures have been reported to exhibit circular dichroism and enhance CPL due to their unique electromagnetic properties, but their effects remain limited. In this study, 3D helical chiral plasmonic meta-clusters are developed by rolling up 2D plasmonic arrays like origami to demonstrate DCP and enhancement of CPL.

k2spring.title_jp: Prediction of Future Afforestation Sites and Sustainable Forest Management in Kyushu
k2spring.abstract_en: Forests serve as a crucial carbon sink. In order to implement afforestation aimed at increasing carbon sequestration and to achieve sustainable forest management, it is essential to consider the impacts of climate change and population decline on forest management. This study predicts suitable areas for afforestation in the latter half of the 21st century, taking into account changes in temperature, precipitation, and population dynamics.

k2spring.title_en: Environmental-economic trade-offs of membrane-based direct air capture technologies using life cycle assessment
k2spring.title_jp: Environmental-economic trade-offs of membrane-based direct air capture technologies using life cycle assessment
k2spring.abstract_en: Climate change is primarily driven by CO₂ emissions. Achieving the Paris Agreement's target of limiting global warming to 1.5 °C requires not only reducing current emissions but also removing existing CO₂ from the atmosphere. This study explores the potential of Direct Air Capture combined with utilisation (DAC-U) at a household scale. Using Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), the research evaluates the environmental and economic feasibility of a small-scale DAC-U system that converts CO₂ into household fuel and generates carbon credits. If environmentally and economically viable, decentralised DAC-U units could provide a socially acceptable means for households to contribute to carbon neutrality.

phd.title_en: Environmental-economic trade-offs of membrane-based direct air capture technologies using life cycle assessment
phd.title_jp: Environmental-economic trade-offs of membrane-based direct air capture technologies using life cycle assessment
phd.abstract_en: Climate change is primarily driven by CO 2 emissions. Achieving the Paris Agreement’s target of limiting global warming to 1.5°C requires not only reducing current emissions but also removing existing CO 2 from the atmosphere. This study explores the potential of Direct Air Capture (DAC) combined with utilization (DAC-U) at a household scale. Using Life Cycle Assessment (LCA), we evaluate the environmental and economic feasibility of a small-scale DAC-U system that converts CO 2 into household fuel and generates carbon credits. The analysis considers energy consumption, fuel production, carbon reduction, and carbon credit trading. If environmentally and economically viable, decentralized DAC-U units could provide a socially acceptable means for households to contribute to carbon neutrality.

k2spring.title_en: Design and Evaluation of PtW-Based Catalysts for Enhancing Fuel Starvation Tolerance in PEFCs
k2spring.title_jp: Design and Evaluation of PtW-Based Catalysts for Enhancing Fuel Starvation Tolerance in PEFCs
k2spring.abstract_en: Polymer electrolyte fuel cells (PEFCs) have attracted attention as a core technology for utilising renewable energy and achieving a zero-emission society. This study focuses on fuel starvation at the anode, one of the major degradation factors in PEFCs. When hydrogen supply is temporarily interrupted, the anode potential rises sharply, leading to irreversible degradation such as carbon support corrosion and platinum catalyst dissolution. These phenomena significantly reduce cell performance and hinder long-term operation. The research aims to design and evaluate PtW-based catalysts with enhanced tolerance to fuel starvation conditions.

k2spring.title_en: CO₂ Reduction Potential of Global Supply Chain Networks: An MRIO Approach Incorporating Maritime Network Structures
k2spring.title_jp: CO₂ Reduction Potential of Global Supply Chain Networks: An MRIO Approach Incorporating Maritime Network Structures
k2spring.abstract_en: This research focuses on the CO₂ reduction potential of global supply chain (GSC) restructuring while considering the environmental efficiency of international shipping. Some 23% of global CO₂ emissions are embodied in traded goods through GSCs. Multi-Regional Input–Output (MRIO) tables — among the most effective tools for analysing GSCs — do not currently capture transportation system data. This study proposes a new MRIO framework that incorporates maritime network structures to estimate the CO₂ reduction potential of supply chain reorganisation.

phd.title_en: CO 2 Reduction Potential of Global Supply Chain Networks: An MRIO Approach Incorporating Maritime Network Structures
phd.title_jp: CO 2 Reduction Potential of Global Supply Chain Networks: An MRIO Approach Incorporating Maritime Network Structures
phd.abstract_en: My research focuses on CO 2 reduction potential of global supply chain (GSC) restructuring with consideration for the environmental efficiency of international shipping. 23% of global CO 2 emissions are embodied in traded goods through GSC. The restructuring of GSC can significantly reduce the carbon footprint of production. However, GSC is highly dependent on international shipping. Multi-Regional Input–Output (MRIO) tables, one of the most effective tools currently for analyzing the GSC, do not provide insights into transportation systems used for trade flows. My research aims to propose a new MRIO framework that incorporate maritime network to estimate the CO 2 reduction potential.

k2spring.title_en: Research on the digital twin of building thermal environment utilizing digital transformation technology
k2spring.title_jp: Research on the digital twin of building thermal environment utilizing digital transformation technology
k2spring.abstract_en: Achieving carbon neutrality by 2050 requires significant efforts in urban and architectural sectors, particularly in accelerating energy efficiency in residential and commercial buildings. This study aims to develop general-purpose software that realises a digital twin of thermal environments using Digital Transformation (DX) technology, focusing on two main objectives: development of a building energy simulation (BES) engine with detailed mathematical models and Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) coupling; and integration of the BES tool with architectural design platforms.

phd.title_en: Research on the digital twin of building thermal environment utilizing digital transformation technology
phd.title_jp: Research on the digital twin of building thermal environment utilizing digital transformation technology
phd.abstract_en: Achieving carbon neutrality by 2050 requires significant efforts in urban and architectural sectors, particularly in accelerating energy efficiency in residential and commercial buildings. This necessitates the development of advanced technologies and highly effective measures. To support this goal, there is a demand for high-precision and versatile numerical analysis tools compatible with cutting-edge technologies. This study aims to develop general-purpose software that realizes a digital twin of thermal environments using DX (Digital Transformation) technology, focusing on two main objectives: Development of an engine for building energy simulation (BES) that supports advanced technologies, including the development of a detailed mathematical model based on building physics and the construction of a coupled analysis method with Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) (Figure 1). Integration of the BES tool with architectural design tools. Figure 1. An example of the coupling method between ES and CFD

k2spring.title_en: Study of F82H precipitate amorphisation in a DEMO fusion reactor
k2spring.title_jp: Study of F82H precipitate amorphisation in a DEMO fusion reactor
k2spring.abstract_en: Fusion reactors are expected to be the next-generation power sources. In the conceptual design of the domestic DEMO reactor, Reduced Activation Ferritic/Martensitic Steel (F82H) is the primary candidate material for the blanket first wall. One of the precipitates in F82H — the Cr₂₃₋ₓWₓC₆ system — has been confirmed to undergo amorphisation under Fe³⁺ ion irradiation at temperatures relevant to fusion reactor operation, raising concerns regarding material degradation. This study aims to elucidate the structural changes leading to amorphisation using first-principles calculations and molecular dynamics simulation.
k2spring.abstract_jp: Fusion reactors are expected to be the next-generation power sources. This study aims to elucidate the structural changes leading to amorphisation using first-principles calculations and molecular dynamics simulation.

phd.title_en: Study of F82H Precipitates Amorphization in DEMO Reactor
phd.title_jp: Study of F82H Precipitates Amorphization in DEMO Reactor
phd.abstract_en: Fusion reactors are expected to be the next-generation power sources. In the conceptual design of the domestic DEMO reactor, the Reduced Activation Ferritic/Martensitic Steel (F82H) is the primary candidate material for the blanket first wall. One of the precipitates present in F82H is the Cr 23-x WxC 6 system, which has been confirmed to be amorphized under Fe 3+ ion irradiation at temperatures relevant to fusion reactor operation, raising potential concerns regarding material degradation. This study aims to elucidate the structural changes leading to the amorphization process using molecular simulation tools such as first-principles calculations and molecular dynamics.

k2spring.title_en: Highly Efficient Ammonia Oxidation by a Macrocyclic Molecular Catalyst
k2spring.title_jp: Highly Efficient Ammonia Oxidation by a Macrocyclic Molecular Catalyst
k2spring.abstract_en: Ammonia has attracted much attention as a new energy carrier. However, efficient catalytic ammonia oxidation to nitrogen has not been well developed, because it involves a challenging six-electron oxidation reaction. Macrocyclic metal complexes such as porphyrins and phthalocyanines have been reported as water oxidation catalysts — which involve a four-electron process — and operate via a bimolecular mechanism. This bimolecular mechanism is important for promoting ammonia oxidation efficiently, since only a three-electron oxidation of the catalyst is required. This study aims to achieve highly efficient ammonia oxidation using a novel macrocyclic molecular catalyst.

This study aims to achieve highly efficient ammonia oxidation using a novel macrocyclic molecular catalyst.

phd.title_en: Highly Efficient Ammonia Oxidation by a Macrocyclic Molecular Catalyst
phd.title_jp: Highly Efficient Ammonia Oxidation by a Macrocyclic Molecular Catalyst
phd.abstract_en: These days, ammonia has attracted much attention as a new energy carrier. However, ammonia oxidation to nitrogen has not been developed well because of 6 electrons oxidation reaction. On the other hand, macrocyclic metal complexes like porphyrins or phthalocyanines have been reported as water oxidation catalysts which is 4 electrons oxidation reaction. These molecular catalysts promote water oxidation via bimolecular mechanism. The bimolecular mechanism is important to promote ammonia oxidation efficiently because 3 electrons oxidation of catalyst is enough. In this study, highly efficient ammonia oxidation is achieved by using a novel macrocyclic molecular catalyst.

k2spring.title_en: The impact mechanism of multi-scale urban built environment on health and well-being of residents
k2spring.title_jp: The impact mechanism of multi-scale urban built environment on health and well-being of residents
k2spring.abstract_en: From an interdisciplinary perspective, this study utilises multivariate data — including remote sensing imagery, street view images, vector data, and house prices — to identify the impact mechanisms of the built environment on public health outcomes (physical, mental, and social health). Machine-learning statistical models including Geographically Weighted Regression, Random Forest, and Latent Growth Curve Modelling are applied at three different scales: city, block, and community. The multi-scale analysis provides quantitative guidance for health-promoting urban planning and design.
k2spring.abstract_jp: From an interdisciplinary perspective, this study utilises multivariate data — including remote sensing imagery, street view images, vector data, and house prices — to identify the impact mechanisms of the built environment on public health outcomes (physical, mental, and social health). Machine-learning statistical models including Geographically Weighted Regression, Random Forest, and Latent Growth Curve Modelling are applied at three different scales: city, block, and community. The multi-scale analysis provides quantitative guidance for health-promoting urban planning and design.

phd.title_en: The impact mechanism of multi-scale urban built environment on health and well-being of residents
phd.title_jp: The impact mechanism of multi-scale urban built environment on health and well-being of residents
phd.abstract_en: From an interdisciplinary perspective, this study utilizes multivariate data such as remote sensing imagery, street view images, vector data, and house prices to identify the impact mechanisms of the built environment on public health outcomes (physical, mental, and social health) , based on the machine-learning statistical models including Geographically Weighted Regression Model, Random Forest Model, and Latent Growth Curve Modeling, respectively, to identify the mechanisms by which the built environment affects public health outcomes (physical, mental, and social health) at three different scales: city, block, and community. Based on the different scales of research, it is possible to obtain more comprehensive findings, which can provide quantitative guidance and insights for health-promoting urban planning and design.

k2spring.title_jp: 機械学習を用いたプロトン伝導性固体酸化物燃料電池カソード材料の加速的探索
k2spring.abstract_en: Proton-conducting solid oxide fuel cells (PCFCs) operating at approximately 300 °C eliminate the need for platinum catalysts. While electrolyte development now enables low-temperature operation, cathode materials remain a performance bottleneck causing output reduction. This research leverages machine learning to accelerate the discovery and optimisation of high-performance PCFC cathode materials.
k2spring.abstract_jp: 約300 °Cで動作するプロトン伝導性固体酸化物燃料電池（PCFC）は白金触媒を不要とします。電解質の開発により低温動作が可能になりましたが、カソード材料が依然として性能のボトルネックとなっています。本研究では機械学習を活用して高性能PCFCカソード材料の探索・最適化を加速します。

k2spring.title_en: Deep-Learning-Based Automated Analysis of Droplet-Impact Splashing Behavior and High-Precision Boundary Estimation
k2spring.title_jp: Deep-Learning-Based Automated Analysis of Droplet-Impact Splashing Behavior and High-Precision Boundary Estimation
k2spring.abstract_en: Conventional analysis of droplet splashing — particularly in the small-droplet regime — has relied on visual inspection and manual measurement, limiting throughput, reproducibility, and objectivity. This project develops a deep-learning pipeline that automatically detects, classifies, and quantifies splashing morphologies and estimates splash boundaries with high precision from high-speed imaging. The framework will be benchmarked against the systematic experimental dataset built in the doctoral research, enabling objective, statistically robust comparison across a wide range of droplet temperatures and ambient pressures, and providing a transferable tool for the broader thermal-fluids community.

k2spring.title_jp: Hybrid Electromagnetic System for Highway Dynamic Charging: S-CPT × Planar Antennas × Energy Harvesting
k2spring.abstract_en: This project goes beyond pure power-transfer optimization by integrating shielded capacitive power transfer (S-CPT) with planar antennas and energy-harvesting elements into a single roadway-embedded electromagnetic system. The aim is to simultaneously realize high-efficiency dynamic charging for in-motion electric vehicles, controlled near-field leakage, and self-powering of roadside sensors. Cross-disciplinary collaboration with antenna and electromagnetic-compatibility researchers within Kyushu University supports unified design of the electrode array, antenna layout, and harvesting subsystem, building a foundation for Japan-led smart-road infrastructure.

k2spring.title_en: Reactive-Step Molecular Dynamics for Mechanistic Understanding of SEI Formation in Lithium-Ion Batteries
k2spring.title_jp: Reactive-Step Molecular Dynamics for Mechanistic Understanding of SEI Formation in Lithium-Ion Batteries
k2spring.abstract_en: Macro-scale battery aging models rely on empirical parameters to describe Solid Electrolyte Interphase (SEI) growth — a picosecond-timescale electrochemical phenomenon that classical molecular dynamics cannot reach and ab-initio MD cannot sustain. This project closes that gap by implementing a reactive-step molecular-dynamics (rs@md) framework that couples classical MD with reaction probabilities derived from transition-state theory. Density-functional theory is used to compute activation barriers for elementary SEI-formation reactions starting from electron transfer at the graphite anode; rs@md simulations of a graphite/electrolyte cell then track SEI film growth and yield effective reaction and Li-ion diffusion rates. The extracted micro-kinetic parameters serve as physics-based inputs to the macroscopic P2D model used in the doctoral research.

k2spring.title_en: First-Principles-Coupled Investigation of Refractory Carbon-Waste Recycling for a New Carbon-Resource Cycle
k2spring.title_jp: First-Principles-Coupled Investigation of Refractory Carbon-Waste Recycling for a New Carbon-Resource Cycle
k2spring.abstract_en: Building on the experiment-driven doctoral work, this project adds a first-principles (DFT) computational pillar to investigate the molecular-scale mechanisms governing the decomposition and liquefaction of refractory carbon waste. The combined experiment + theory approach clarifies elementary reaction pathways, identifies catalytically active sites, and predicts how feedstock composition and process conditions control yield, selectivity, and downstream carbon-material properties. Cross-disciplinary collaboration with computational-chemistry and reaction-engineering groups within Kyushu University provides the human-sciences perspective that K 2 -SPRING emphasizes, with the goal of constructing a new carbon-circulation paradigm for Japan.

k2spring.title_en: Advancing Floating Offshore Wind Turbines via Fusion of Fluid Physics and Control Theory: Heave-Plate Damping Mechanism
k2spring.title_jp: Advancing Floating Offshore Wind Turbines via Fusion of Fluid Physics and Control Theory: Heave-Plate Damping Mechanism
k2spring.abstract_en: Standard control models of floating offshore wind turbines treat heave-plate hydrodynamic forces with simplified drag coefficients, but real sea states are dominated by complex vortex-shedding phenomena that conventional formulations cannot capture. This project deliberately steps outside the doctoral 'control/theory' framework to interrogate the underlying fluid-physics — specifically the damping mechanism driven by vortex shedding around heave plates in the low Keulegan–Carpenter (KC) regime. CFD simulations (Code_Saturne) carried out at Kyushu University are paired with tank experiments at ENSTA Paris (Institut Polytechnique de Paris), under a collaboration with Prof. Luc Pastur initiated by the applicant. PIV-based visualization quantifies vortex shedding under irregular waves and at low frequencies. The outcome is a more general, physics-grounded heave-plate design formula that can be embedded as a parameter in the doctoral state-space model, closing the loop between physical understanding and control design.

k2spring.title_en: Thermo-Informatics Elucidation of Heat-Transfer Enhancement Mechanisms in Flow Boiling
k2spring.title_jp: Thermo-Informatics Elucidation of Heat-Transfer Enhancement Mechanisms in Flow Boiling
k2spring.abstract_en: Flow boiling is a fundamental physical phenomenon in heat-exchanger design, yet its complex multiphase, multiscale heat-transfer mechanisms remain only partially systematized. This project uses thermo-informatics — the fusion of thermal engineering and information science — to organize heat-transfer mechanisms in flow boiling. By combining experimental observation with data-driven analysis, the project aims to deliver foundational knowledge that supports refrigerant transition for environmental conservation and informs the design of next-generation heat exchangers in the refrigeration and HVAC field.

k2spring.title_en: MS-SOFC Cell-Stack Fabrication and Structural Evaluation Toward Practical Implementation
k2spring.title_jp: MS-SOFC Cell-Stack Fabrication and Structural Evaluation Toward Practical Implementation
k2spring.abstract_en: Conventional metal-supported solid-oxide fuel cell (MS-SOFC) research has remained at the single-cell stage, leaving the stack-level — multi-cell, multi-layer — challenges of mechanical and electrochemical integration largely open. This project builds on the applicant's master-level work on co-sintered MS-SOFC fabrication and extends it to the cell-stack level: stack-architecture design, structural evaluation under operational thermal and redox cycling, and reliability assessment under wet-oxidizing and reducing atmospheres. The work targets compact, durable distributed-power and portable-power applications and provides the manufacturing-process knowledge required for industrial implementation of next-generation SOFC technology.

k2spring.title_en: Elucidating PFAS Transport and Adsorption–Desorption Properties in Soil
k2spring.title_jp: Elucidating PFAS Transport and Adsorption–Desorption Properties in Soil
k2spring.abstract_en: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) — the so-called 'forever chemicals' — are now recognized as a global environmental and public-health concern, yet their transport, adsorption, and desorption behavior in soil systems remains insufficiently characterized for reliable site assessment and remediation design. This project combines column-experiment data acquisition with reactive-transport modeling to quantify PFAS migration and partitioning across representative soil compositions, water-saturation conditions, and organic-matter contents. The work leverages porous-media transport methods used in the doctoral PEMWE research and applies them — across disciplines, in line with the spirit of K 2 -SPRING — to environmental remediation, with the aim of supporting evidence-based PFAS management in Japan.

k2spring.title_en: Numerical Modeling of Hydrogen Behavior in Pd–Ag Plasma Electrodes for Fusion Fuel-Cycle Systems
k2spring.title_jp: Numerical Modeling of Hydrogen Behavior in Pd–Ag Plasma Electrodes for Fusion Fuel-Cycle Systems
k2spring.abstract_en: Complementing the experimental doctoral work, this project constructs a numerical model that resolves the sequence of processes by which hydrogen ions and atoms enter the plasma sheath near a palladium–silver electrode, are absorbed, diffuse through the alloy, and are released on the opposite surface. A one-dimensional diffusion–reaction model is coupled with a plasma sheath model that supplies incident ion fluxes, ion energies, and sputtering yields; sputtering-induced erosion, redeposition, and surface composition changes are incorporated. Comparison with experimentally measured hydrogen-permeation fluxes and electrode-composition changes identifies rate-limiting steps and operating conditions that maximize hydrogen-isotope recovery. Collaboration with researchers in materials modeling and computational chemistry refines material parameters.

k2spring.title_en: Photothermal Nanoparticle-Enhanced Liquid Evaporation for Solar-Driven Energy and Water Harvest
k2spring.title_jp: Photothermal Nanoparticle-Enhanced Liquid Evaporation for Solar-Driven Energy and Water Harvest
k2spring.abstract_en: This project investigates how photothermal (plasmonic) nanoparticles redistribute and deposit during solar-driven liquid evaporation, and how the resulting deposition morphology governs optical absorption, internal Marangoni and capillary flows, and overall evaporation efficiency. Quantitative microPIV/PTV/LIF experiments map the flow dynamics of nanoparticle-laden droplets under solar radiation and the three-dimensional pattern formation of drying colloidal droplets. A finite-element computational framework is developed to capture plasmonic heating, thermocapillary flow, and the resulting deposition. Modified nanoparticles and Patterned Liquid-Like Surfaces are then engineered to suppress contact-line pinning and enable controlled deposition. Collaboration with the Yukina Takahashi laboratory at I 2 CNER provides photoelectrochemical-materials expertise.

k2spring.title_en: Specifying Proton-Conductor Requirements for Stable Mass Production of Solid Oxide Fuel Cells
k2spring.title_jp: Specifying Proton-Conductor Requirements for Stable Mass Production of Solid Oxide Fuel Cells
k2spring.abstract_en: This project bridges fundamental materials science and the social implementation of solid oxide fuel cells (SOFCs). Using insights from non-perovskite proton conductors, the work defines the required materials parameters — proton concentration, diffusion behavior, chemical and mechanical stability — that proton-conductor electrolytes must satisfy to enable stable, low-cost SOFC mass production. Cross-field discussion with cell-fabrication and process-engineering groups translates atomic-scale defect-chemistry knowledge into design guidelines and quality-control criteria, supporting Japan's hydrogen-society roadmap.

k2spring.title_en: Design of an Electron-Rich Co–NHC Catalyst for Mild-Condition Nitrogen Reduction
k2spring.title_jp: Design of an Electron-Rich Co–NHC Catalyst for Mild-Condition Nitrogen Reduction
k2spring.abstract_en: Industrial ammonia synthesis still relies on the Haber–Bosch process, which operates at 500–600 °C and 200–500 atm and consumes 1–2% of global energy while emitting more than 300 Mt of CO 2 annually. This project designs an electron-rich Co–N-heterocyclic carbene (Co–NHC) catalyst capable of reducing dinitrogen to ammonia under mild photocatalytic / electrocatalytic conditions. By tuning the electronic structure at the cobalt center through tailored NHC ligands, the work seeks to weaken the N≡N triple bond at low temperature and pressure and provide a viable, low-energy route to nitrogen fixation, complementing — but distinct in catalyst class from — the doctoral hydrogen-evolution research.

k2spring.title_en: Machine-Learning-Enhanced Reactive Molecular Dynamics for Ammonia Combustion
k2spring.title_jp: Machine-Learning-Enhanced Reactive Molecular Dynamics for Ammonia Combustion
k2spring.abstract_en: This project couples machine learning with reactive molecular dynamics (ReaxFF MD) to overcome the accuracy and computational limits of conventional empirical force fields in ammonia (NH 3 ) combustion modeling. Deep neural networks are used to construct high-precision machine-learning potential energy surfaces, replacing empirical potentials and enabling long-timescale simulations of complex reaction networks. ML algorithms are then applied to large reaction-trajectory datasets to identify key intermediates, activation-energy variations, and dominant reaction pathways, with a particular focus on the formation mechanism of NO x . Extracted micro-kinetic parameters are fed back into the reaction model to globally optimize combustion pathways. Interdisciplinary collaboration with ISEE (deep-learning architecture optimization) and IGSES peers (CFD and engine experiments) builds a multiscale workflow connecting atomistic kinetics to engine-scale combustion.
